sn36.21:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha in the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrel’s Sanctuary.
sn36.21:1.2: There Moḷiyasivaka the wanderer went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn36.21:1.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
sn36.21:1.4: “Master Gotama, there are some contemplatives & brahmans who are of this doctrine, this view:
sn36.21:1.5: Whatever an individual feels—pleasure, pain, neither-pleasure-nor-pain—is entirely caused by what was done before.
sn36.21:1.6: Now what does Master Gotama say to that?”
sn36.21:2.1: [The Buddha:] “There are cases where some feelings arise based on bile [i.e., diseases and pains that come from a malfunction of the gall bladder].
sn36.21:2.2: You yourself should know how some feelings arise based on bile.
sn36.21:2.3: Even the world is agreed on how some feelings arise based on bile.
sn36.21:2.4: So any contemplatives & brahmans who are of the doctrine & view that
sn36.21:2.5: whatever an individual feels—pleasure, pain, neither-pleasure-nor-pain—is entirely caused by what was done before—
sn36.21:2.6: slip past what they themselves know, slip past what is agreed on by the world.
sn36.21:2.7: Therefore I say that those contemplatives & brahmans are wrong.”
sn36.21:3.1: “There are cases where some feelings arise based on phlegm…
sn36.21:3.3: based on a combination of bodily humors…
sn36.21:3.4: from the change of the seasons…
sn36.21:3.5: from uneven [‘out-of-tune’] care of the body…
sn36.21:3.9: how some feelings arise from the result of kamma.
sn36.21:3.10: Even the world is agreed on
sn36.21:3.11: how some feelings arise from the result of kamma.
sn36.21:3.12: So any contemplatives & brahmans who are of the doctrine & view that
sn36.21:3.13: whatever an individual feels—pleasure, pain, neither pleasure-nor-pain—is entirely caused by what was done before—
sn36.21:3.14: slip past what they themselves know, slip past what is agreed on by the world.
sn36.21:3.15: Therefore I say that those contemplatives & brahmans are wrong.”
sn36.21:4.1: When this was said, Moḷiyasivaka the wanderer said to the Blessed One:
sn36.21:4.2: Magnificent, lord! Magnificent! Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to point out the way to one who was lost, or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms, in the same way has the Blessed One—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear. I go to the Blessed One for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
sn36.21:4.3: May the Blessed One remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.
sn36.21:5.4: and through the result of kamma as the eighth.”
